question: Why was each decision made?
title: Decision history
summary: Rejected approaches matter as much as chosen ones—they stop the same debate in every new AI session.

scenes.headline: Decisions are not footnotes. They are guardrails.
scenes.body: When Cursor suggests MySQL, auth, or uploads again, the answer is already in the repository—with the reasoning that convinced you last time.

scenes.label: Major decisions in order
scenes.description: Expand any decision to see context, rejected options, and what would change your mind.

decisions.title: SQLite instead of MySQL
decisions.outcome: File-based database beside the app on cPanel.
decisions.context: Single operator, simple backup, local dev matches production. MySQL adds hosting complexity without a concurrency requirement.

rejected.reason: Extra service to manage for one writer.
No multi-user signal yet; premature optimization.

consequences: Engine change requires migration decision file

decisions.title: No authentication in v1
decisions.outcome: One trusted operator; no user table.
decisions.context: Weekend tool for personal ideas. Auth would dominate scope without a collaboration requirement.

rejected.reason: Halfway auth is worse than none—documented trap.

consequences: Do not expose to untrusted networks
Multi-user is a new project phase, not a patch

decisions.title: Considered PostgreSQL, stayed on SQLite
decisions.outcome: Export plans did not justify a database migration.
decisions.context: AI suggested PostgreSQL during export discussion. Operator count unchanged; backup story still simpler with one file.

rejected.reason: Same as day-one rejection; export works from SQLite.

consequences: Revisit only with real multi-user need

decisions.title: Manual migrations
decisions.outcome: SQL files applied by operator; no migration runner.
decisions.context: Tiny schema, cPanel constraints, full visibility over changes.

rejected.reason: Adds framework weight; hides deploy steps.
Dangerous on production without explicit apply.

consequences: Every schema change needs a migration file + guide note
Status field incident taught schema-before-code lesson
